title: Porpax jerdoniana
intro: Porpax jerdoniana, also known as Porpax, is a species of orchid found in the Western Ghats of India, characterized by its unique, terrestrial habit and small, white or yellowish flowers. Traditionally, Porpax is used in Ayurvedic medicine, where it is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Porpax include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In Ayurvedic practice, Porpax is often prepared as a decoction or churna (powder), and is also used in the form of a paste or oil to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Porpax jerdoniana is classified within the family Orchidaceae. This classification places it within the larger group of flowering plants. More specifically, it belongs to the subfamily Epidendroideae. Further classification also includes its position within the tribe Gastrodieae. Additionally, its genus Porpax is often grouped together with other closely related genera.
